rows.description: Computus Dni Rectoris a 2do De Novr 1814
rows.notes: Top-of-page title. Ink fairly clear; no amounts present.
Continuation of heading beneath first line; no monetary marks.
Section label. Handwriting slightly slanted but legible; no amounts.
Section label introducing fixed rents; no amounts on this row.

Amount '135' is a clear three-digit number in the pounds column. Description handwriting slightly uncertain (interpreted as 'Grincar'); column boundaries set by aligning with clear totals lower on page.
Amount '136' clearly in pounds column. Description letters ambiguous but context (second fixed rent) makes reading plausible. Column alignment referenced to final totals.
No monetary figures visible on this line; appears to be a sub-item or note under the fixed rents. Handwriting faded for the description.
A single '10' appears rightward but aligned under the shillings band when columns projected from the final totals. Interpreted as 10 shillings (0 pounds, 10s). Description uncertain (handwriting).
Section heading; clearly written and contains no amounts.
Name of rectory appears but no amount on the same line; the following line ('Vicarage') carries the monetary figure.
Amount reads as '4.19.' — interpreted as 4 pounds and 19 shillings (no pence). Alignment checked against lower totals; shillings 19 is within valid range.
Section label for variable rents; clear handwriting and no amounts on this row.
Amount appears as '171.11.' — interpreted as 171 pounds, 11 shillings, no pence. Alignment taken from the final totals; digits are clear though the final pence column is blank.
Amount reads as '42.9.4 1/2' (the halfpenny marked by a loop/abbreviation). Column boundaries set against page totals; fraction interpreted as 0.5 (halfpenny).
Amount '10.4.11' is legible and fits the columns determined from totals. Pence 11 is within 0–11 rule.
Reads as '9.11.' — interpreted as 9 pounds, 11 shillings, pence blank. Alignment validated against totals.
Line shows as '8 . - 1' (i.e. 8 pounds, 0 shillings, 1 pence). Read using column boundaries from final totals; pence digit legible.
Appears as '44.2.' — interpreted as 44 pounds, 2 shillings. Columns verified with final totals alignment.
Subsection heading 'Little Tew' is clear and centered; no amounts on this row.
Amount '28.7.11' is clear; used final total alignment to verify column placement of 28 (pounds), 7 (shillings), 11 (pence).
Reads '13.15.6' clearly. All currency components within valid ranges.
Description uncertain (hand-lettering); amount '12.19.10' legible and validated against column guides. Chosen reading over close alternatives based on digit shapes.
Amount '3.13.0' readable; columns matched to page totals. Description 'Hartley' is clear.
Amount '24.3.10' readable; alignment consistent with lower totals. Description legible.
Amount appears as '36.12.1 1/2' — pence shows a small loop indicating a halfpenny. Column alignment taken from final totals; fraction read as 0.5.
Subsection heading (Lad Lane) is clear; no amounts on this row.
Reads '8.8.' — interpreted as 8 pounds and 8 shillings. Verified against the column positions projected from the page totals.
Line shows '6.' in the pounds column with trailing dashes in the shillings/pence columns; interpreted as 6 pounds exactly. Alignment confirmed with totals.
Clearly reads '1.10.' — 1 pound 10 shillings. Column positions unambiguous relative to totals.
Appears as '6.' in the pounds column (no shillings/pence). Cleanly aligned with final totals guidance.
Clear final total on the receipts block '613.19.1'. Used as primary alignment reference for all amount columns on the page; digits are crisp and unambiguous.
Section heading beneath the main receipts total; handwriting clear and centered, no amounts on this line.
Line lists 'Received of Bankers' with the first dividend amount visible as '9.3.8'. Columns positioned using the principal totals; digits are distinct.
Second banker dividend reads '19.3.8'. Read with the same alignment; digits clear and validate (shillings/pence in range).
Final small total under banker receipts reads clearly as '38.7.4'. Used as secondary alignment check; digits crisp and unambiguous.
